summary: Every periodic wave obeys v = λ·f: its speed equals wavelength times frequency. Use it to move between how long, how often, and how fast.
method: Identify the wave and the two known quantities.
Decide which quantity is unknown — speed, wavelength, or frequency.
Rearrange v = λ·f for the unknown.
Use SI units (m/s, m, Hz); convert wavelengths in nm or cm to metres first.

example.title: Wavelength of a sound wave
steps: The unknown is λ, so use λ = v/f.
